what_it_does: Filesystem-backed addressed store implementing the resolver contract: content-addressed immutable JSON and binary objects, mutable refs with version history, provenance and lineage, memo gate, graph/session/journey/agent-session/chat-thread/scale-pyramid records, append-only event/chat/annotation/finding/disposition/mark/pin/agent-mail logs, surfaced decision records, and a cached vector index with per-space cosine matrices. It hides filesystem concurrency and durability (fcntl flock, RLock, fsync, atomic rename) behind a backend-agnostic store seam.
observations: Implements both cas:// JSON objects and blob:// raw binary objects in the same objects/ namespace, both using blake2b 16-byte content addresses.
set_ref writes the live ref atomically and appends a version-history jsonl line, so every write is recorded without copying the bytes.
Graph mutations are protected by per-graph re-entrant cross-process locks composed of a thread RLock and an fcntl.flock file lock.
Atomic durable writes use pid+thread-unique temp files, os.replace, fsync of the temp file, and fsync of the parent directory.
The vector subsystem keeps an incremental (mtime_ns,size)-validated record cache, a per-(space,emb) numpy matrix cache, and an mtime-gated layer self-description memo.
Append-only logs (events, chat, annotations, findings, dispositions, marks, pins, agent mail) are read from disk on every call, so a second process sees writes immediately.
Agent mail uses the cross-process graph_lock around the read-last-seq/increment/append step and fsyncs each append for intent-class durability.
Surfaced decisions have a store-level RLock so the Suite and governance callers can serialize read-modify-write cycles.
standouts: The _CrossProcessLock composition of thread RLock + fcntl.flock with per-thread nesting depth is the central concurrency primitive.
The extensive inline commentary justifying every design choice around portability, durability, cross-process correctness, and additive schema evolution.
The vector subsystem's layered caching (record cache, matrix cache, layers memo) avoids repeated full-directory parses while remaining cross-process correct.
The agent-mail cursor design uses per-consumer durable refs instead of mutating messages, making consumption positions explicit and replay-safe.
conventions: All durable pointer writes go through _atomic_write_fsync (tmp+fsync+replace).
Append-only jsonl logs with field-match retrieval; last-wins overlays for mutable state (pins, dispositions).
Local imports of json and datetime inside methods keep the module top-level minimal.
Fail-loud structural validation for empty ids, missing mail fields, and spaced vectors without a source.
Field-portable design: space, source, emb, target, and mark_type are explicit record fields rather than values parsed out of address strings.
Comments cite design-rule codes such as T1-SEQ, X12-FAST, S4, and L6.
concerns: The _safe filename encoder can collide (for example, ':' and '_' both map to '_'), though the code treats this as the address-safe encoder.
append_event seq uniqueness is explicitly NOT cross-process, per the surfaced note in the method.
Many hot-path reads (annotations_for, chats_for, agent_mail_since, all_findings, all_marks) read and parse the entire append-only file on every call, so unbounded growth will slow them.
warm_vector_cache and space_matrix silently swallow exceptions, which could mask numpy or import failures.
agent_mail_since loads the whole mail.jsonl into memory; the keep-forever design may eventually cause latency.
Local repeated imports of json and datetime in many methods add small overhead and noise.
notes: This is the filesystem substrate that every higher-level store operation funnels through; its main job is to make concurrent, durable, content-addressed storage look like a simple address-to-bytes seam.
questions: Is the _safe collision risk acceptable for all address grammars used in the project?
Are there plans to bound or paginate append-only logs (chat, annotations, findings, marks, mail) as they grow?
Will a future Supabase backend reuse the same cross-process lock semantics, or will the graph_lock contract change?
Why do put_content and put_blob not fsync the object bytes, while refs and graphs do?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the docstring says it implements a filesystem-backed addressed store with concurrency and durability behind the resolver seam, and the code does exactly that across objects, refs, logs, graphs, vectors, and surfaced records.
apparent_themes: Store as a dumb, address-to-bytes seam with semantics living above it.
Crash durability and cross-process correctness as first-class constraints.
Additive, append-only, immutable records with overlays for mutable state.
Backend portability: filesystem specifics hidden behind method contracts.
Performance through incremental caching without sacrificing cross-process correctness.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be the single, portable, durable substrate that can be swapped to a non-filesystem backend without changing callers, while surviving multi-process/multi-thread races and power-loss.
novelty: The combination of a re-entrant cross-process lock primitive, an mtime-gated vector self-description memo, and per-consumer durable mail cursors in one filesystem store is unusual for a small Python module.
contribution: A concrete, well-documented filesystem implementation of the resolver/store contract that centralizes atomicity, durability, and concurrency primitives, and provides a broad set of addressed storage leaves (objects, refs, logs, vectors, decisions, mail) that a fused system can keep as the reference substrate or reimplement behind the same API.
summary_for_embedding: company/store/fs_store.py is a filesystem-backed addressed store providing content-addressed immutable objects, mutable refs, provenance, append-only logs, graph/session/vector/decision records, and cross-process locking with atomic fsync writes.
intention_for_embedding: To be the durable, concurrent, backend-portable storage seam that all higher-level code uses via addresses rather than filesystem paths.
suggested_fields: backend_seam_contracts: methods that must be reimplemented by a non-filesystem backend
durability_guarantees: which writes are fsynced versus atomic-only
cross_process_lock_scope: keys protected by graph_lock, dispatch_lock, and agent_mail_seq
append_only_log_files: paths and retrieval-key fields for each log
vector_cache_invalidation_signals: mtime and version bump triggers

symbols.does: Composes a thread RLock with an fcntl.flock file lock for re-entrant cross-process serialization.
symbols.description: Tracks per-thread nesting depth so the OS flock is taken only on the outermost acquire and released only on the outermost exit, giving re-entrancy within one thread while still serializing across processes.

symbols.does: Initializes the lockfile path, in-process RLock, depth/fd maps, and the guard lock.
symbols.description: Sets up the per-thread bookkeeping used to decide when to actually acquire and release the cross-process fcntl lock.

symbols.does: Context manager that acquires and releases the composed lock re-entrantly.
symbols.description: Uses the RLock for in-process serialization, then records nesting depth under a guard; only the outermost enter opens the lockfile and takes an exclusive flock, and only the outermost exit releases it.

symbols.does: Best-effort fsync of a directory entry to harden rename durability.
symbols.description: Opens the parent directory read-only and fsyncs it; swallows errors on platforms or filesystems that disallow directory fsync.

symbols.does: Atomically and durably writes a string to a file.
symbols.description: Writes to a pid+thread-unique temp file, fsyncs the temp fd, atomically renames it over the target, then fsyncs the parent directory so the rename survives a crash.

symbols.does: Filesystem-backed addressed store implementing the resolver contract.
symbols.description: Manages directories for objects, refs, graphs, vectors, surfaced decisions, sessions, journeys, agent sessions, chat threads, and append-only logs; provides atomic durable writes and cross-process locking.

symbols.does: Creates store directories and initializes locks and vector caches.
symbols.description: Ensures all substrate directories exist, sets up store-level RLocks, the per-graph cross-process lock map, and the in-memory vector and layers caches.

symbols.does: Produces a filename-safe lockfile name from an arbitrary key.
symbols.description: Reuses the address-safe encoding so graph ids and dispatch-claim keys become valid filenames under locks/.

symbols.does: Returns a re-entrant cross-process lock context manager for a graph id.
symbols.description: Creates a _CrossProcessLock on demand under a guard and returns its acquire context, serializing whole-graph read-modify-write mutations across threads and processes.

symbols.does: Alias for the dispatch-claim cross-process lock.
symbols.description: Routes to graph_lock with a dispatch-claim: prefix so exactly-once dispatch check-claim uses the same cross-process primitive.

symbols.does: Hashes bytes into a cas:// blake2b address.
symbols.description: Returns a content-address string with a cas://b2: scheme prefix using a 16-byte blake2b digest.

symbols.does: Encodes an address string into a safe filename.
symbols.description: Replaces ://, :, and / with underscores so arbitrary addresses can be used as filesystem paths.

symbols.does: Persists a JSON-serializable object as an immutable CAS object.
symbols.description: Hashes the canonical JSON bytes, writes them once to objects/ if absent, and fails loudly if the target path is a directory.

symbols.does: Reads and deserializes a CAS object by address.
symbols.description: Returns the JSON value stored under the safe-encoded CAS address.

symbols.does: Checks whether a CAS object is present.
symbols.description: Returns True if the safe-encoded CAS file exists in objects/.

symbols.does: Hashes raw bytes into a blob:// blake2b address.
symbols.description: Like _hash but prefixes with blob:// to distinguish binary objects from JSON CAS objects.

symbols.does: Persists raw bytes as an immutable blob object.
symbols.description: Hashes the bytes, writes them once to objects/ if absent, and rejects non-bytes input with a clear error.

symbols.does: Reads a blob object by address.
symbols.description: Returns the raw bytes stored under the safe-encoded blob address.

symbols.does: Checks whether a blob object is present.
symbols.description: Returns True if the safe-encoded blob file exists in objects/.

symbols.does: Method alias to the module-level atomic write primitive.
symbols.description: Routes self._fsync_atomic_write calls to _atomic_write_fsync so there is one durable write implementation.

symbols.does: Atomically sets a mutable run:// reference to a CAS address.
symbols.description: Writes the reference atomically via _fsync_atomic_write, then appends a version-history entry to ref_history/.

symbols.does: Appends a timestamped CAS entry to a ref's version history.
symbols.description: Writes one JSON line to a per-logical-ref jsonl file using an O_APPEND open, recording every set_ref write.

symbols.does: Returns the ordered version trail of a logical ref.
symbols.description: Reads the per-ref jsonl, optionally limits to the newest N entries, and returns oldest-first {ts, cas} records.

symbols.does: Returns the current value of a mutable reference.
symbols.description: Reads the ref file if it exists; otherwise returns None.

symbols.does: Atomically writes a Provenance record.
symbols.description: Serializes the provenance object to JSON and writes it durably under meta/, keyed by the safe-encoded address.

symbols.does: Reads a Provenance record by address.
symbols.description: Validates the JSON meta file against the Provenance model if present, else returns None.

symbols.does: Walks provenance inputs back to sources.
symbols.description: Performs a breadth-first, de-duplicated, cycle-safe traversal of provenance input links.

symbols.does: Reads a memo gate value by signature.
symbols.description: Returns the CAS address stored under memo/ for the given signature, or None.

symbols.does: Atomically writes a memo gate value.
symbols.description: Stores the CAS address for a signature using the durable atomic write primitive.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a Graph record.
symbols.description: Serializes the graph to JSON and writes it durably under graphs/ using a unique temp file and fsync.

symbols.does: Loads a Graph record by id.
symbols.description: Reads and validates the graphs/ JSON file if it exists, else returns None.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a review-session record.
symbols.description: Writes a session dict to sessions/ using the same crash-durable atomic write pattern.

symbols.does: Loads a review-session record by id.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the sessions/ JSON file if present.

symbols.does: Lists persisted review-session ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in sessions/.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a scale-pyramid structure.
symbols.description: Writes a pyramid dict to scale/, optionally keyed by embedder tag so different layers do not overwrite each other.

symbols.does: Loads a scale-pyramid record by space and optional embedder.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the scale/ JSON file if present.

symbols.does: Lists persisted scale-pyramid keys.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in scale/.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a journey record.
symbols.description: Writes a journey dict to journeys/ using the durable atomic write pattern.

symbols.does: Loads a journey record by id.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the journeys/ JSON file if present.

symbols.does: Lists persisted journey ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in journeys/.

symbols.does: Atomically persists an agent-session registry record.
symbols.description: Validates that the record has a non-empty id, then writes it durably to agent_sessions/.

symbols.does: Loads an agent-session registry record by id.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the agent_sessions/ JSON file if present.

symbols.does: Lists persisted agent-session ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in agent_sessions/.

symbols.does: Returns a map of agent-session ids to record file mtime.
symbols.description: Used for cheap change detection so consumers reload only records that have changed.

symbols.does: Atomically persists an agent-session timeline cache record.
symbols.description: Writes a timeline dict to agent_sessions/timeline/ after validating the session id.

symbols.does: Loads an agent-session timeline cache record by id.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the agent_sessions/timeline/ JSON file if present.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a chat-thread metadata record.
symbols.description: Writes a thread dict to chat_threads/ using the durable atomic write pattern.

symbols.does: Loads a chat-thread metadata record by id.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the chat_threads/ JSON file if present.

symbols.does: Lists persisted chat-thread ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in chat_threads/.

symbols.does: Returns chat turns belonging to a thread.
symbols.description: Filters the shared append-only chat.jsonl by thread_id and returns the oldest-first last limit turns.

symbols.does: Lists persisted graph ids.
symbols.description: Returns sorted stems of all .json files in graphs/.

symbols.does: Appends a uniquely sequenced event to events.jsonl.
symbols.description: Under a thread RLock, reads the last seq, increments, writes the record with fsync; notes cross-process uniqueness is not enforced.

symbols.does: Returns the newest N events.
symbols.description: Reads events.jsonl and returns the last limit records in newest-first order.

symbols.does: Returns events with seq greater than a cursor.
symbols.description: Reads events.jsonl and returns matching records oldest-first, used as an SSE stream cursor.

symbols.does: Appends a chat turn to chat.jsonl.
symbols.description: Adds a timestamped record to the shared append-only chat log.

symbols.does: Returns the last N chat turns.
symbols.description: Reads chat.jsonl and returns the oldest-first last limit records.

symbols.does: Returns chat turns attached to a ui:// address.
symbols.description: Filters the shared chat.jsonl by the address field, isolating the conversation thread at that locus.

symbols.does: Appends an annotation to annotations.jsonl.
symbols.description: Writes a timestamped open record to the separate annotations log; does not validate the address.

symbols.does: Returns annotations attached to a ui:// address.
symbols.description: Filters annotations.jsonl by address and returns oldest-first records.

symbols.does: Appends a coherence finding to findings.jsonl.
symbols.description: Writes a timestamped open record to the findings append-only log.

symbols.does: Returns findings at a given address.
symbols.description: Filters findings.jsonl by address and returns oldest-first records.

symbols.does: Returns every finding record.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the entire findings.jsonl for rollup or burn-down consumers.

symbols.does: Appends a disposition record to dispositions.jsonl.
symbols.description: Records a decision on a (kind, address) finding handle; later records override earlier ones on read.

symbols.does: Returns the latest disposition for a finding handle.
symbols.description: Scans dispositions.jsonl and returns the last record matching the given kind and address.

symbols.does: Appends a mark to marks.jsonl.
symbols.description: Validates target and mark_type, then writes a timestamped open record to the separate marks leaf so marks never pollute findings.

symbols.does: Returns marks for a target.
symbols.description: Filters marks.jsonl by target and returns oldest-first records.

symbols.does: Returns marks of a given mark_type.
symbols.description: Filters marks.jsonl by mark_type, providing the cross-target view of one mark kind.

symbols.does: Returns every mark record.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the entire marks.jsonl for cross-mark rollups.

symbols.does: Appends a pin-state record to pins.jsonl.
symbols.description: Records a pinned or unpinned overlay for an (address, target_ts) item handle without mutating the underlying annotation or chat log.

symbols.does: Returns resolved pin state for an address.
symbols.description: Scans pins.jsonl and returns the latest pinned boolean per target_ts for the given address.

symbols.does: Composes a vector storage address from source, space, and embedder.
symbols.description: Builds vec:// addresses with optional #space and #emb fragments; returns the bare source when both are absent for backward compatibility.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a vector entry.
symbols.description: Writes a record with address, vector, content_hash, dim, model, space, source, and emb to vectors/ using the durable atomic write primitive.

symbols.does: Loads a vector entry by address.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the vectors/ JSON file if present; returns None for missing entries.

symbols.does: Removes a vector entry by address.
symbols.description: Unlinks the vectors/ JSON file if it exists, returning True if removed; the next cache scan evicts it.

symbols.does: Bulk removes vector entries.
symbols.description: Calls remove_vector for each address and returns the count actually removed.

symbols.does: Returns all vector records using an incremental in-memory cache.
symbols.description: Scans vectors/, revalidates each file by (mtime_ns, size), parses only changed or new files, evicts removed files, and bumps the matrix version on any change.

symbols.does: Pre-populates vector and matrix caches at startup.
symbols.description: Calls _vector_records and pre-builds numpy matrices for large named spaces; best-effort and never blocks startup on failure.

symbols.does: Lists addresses in the vector index, optionally filtered by space.
symbols.description: Reads via the cached _vector_records and returns sorted addresses; default space=None returns only unspaced entries.

symbols.does: Returns the vector corpus in retrieve.run shape.
symbols.description: Filters cached vector records by space and emb and returns {id, vector} entries; supports a pre-fetched records snapshot for multi-space queries.

symbols.does: Builds and caches a per-(space,emb) numpy cosine matrix.
symbols.description: Returns (ids, M) for fast matmul cosine ranking, keyed on _vec_version so any vector change invalidates it; returns None for default or ALL spaces or ragged dims.

symbols.does: Returns which embedder layers exist per space.
symbols.description: Served from the mtime-gated self-description memo, excluding default and scale:* spaces.

symbols.does: Builds the mtime-gated layers and dims memo.
symbols.description: Uses one vectors-dir mtime stat as a gate and recomputes layers and dims from _vector_records only when a file has been added or removed.

symbols.does: Returns vector dimensions per (space, embedder-layer).
symbols.description: Served from the same mtime-gated memo as layers_by_space, used to derive MRL zoom ladders.

symbols.does: Returns the store-level surfaced RLock.
symbols.description: Callers hold this around get_surfaced-mutate-save_surfaced to prevent lost updates across Suite and governance writers.

symbols.does: Atomically persists a surfaced decision record.
symbols.description: Under the surfaced lock, writes the decision dict to surfaced/ using the durable atomic write primitive.

symbols.does: Lists all surfaced decision records.
symbols.description: Reads and parses every .json file in surfaced/ in sorted order.

symbols.does: Loads a surfaced decision by id.
symbols.description: Reads and parses the surfaced/ JSON file if present.

symbols.does: Appends a uniquely sequenced fabric mail record.
symbols.description: Validates to, from, verb, and cas, assigns seq/id/ts/thread under a cross-process graph_lock, and fsyncs the append for intent-class durability.

symbols.does: Returns mail records after a seq cursor with optional filters.
symbols.description: Reads agent_sessions/mail.jsonl oldest-first, filtering by to, verb, thread, and limit; fresh consumers pass -1.

symbols.does: Reads a durable per-consumer mail cursor.
symbols.description: Returns the int value stored in the ref agent-mail-cursor://<consumer>, or -1; fails loudly on corrupt values.

symbols.does: Advances a durable per-consumer mail cursor.
symbols.description: Under a per-consumer cross-process lock, refuses backward moves, then sets the ref and records the advance in ref_history.
